text: HTTPS encrypts data between the user's browser and the website, protecting sensitive information like passwords and credit card numbers. Authentication, such as strong passwords or two-factor verification, ensures only authorized users can access accounts. Input validation is critical—developers must check that user inputs follow expected formats, preventing malicious code from being inserted. Common vulnerabilities include cross-site scripting (XSS) and SQL injection, where attackers exploit weak security to steal data or take control of systems. By implementing these measures, developers can build safer web applications.
